text: During the cholecystectomy, the surgeon identifies the cystic duct and cystic artery after dissecting the posterior wall of the gallbladder up the fossa. The cystic duct is seen inserting onto the infundibulum of the gallbladder. As the dissection progresses, another duct structure becomes visible, highlighted under firefly mode due to preoperative endocyanin green administration.

The surgeon continues dissection to isolate an aberrant duct structure, using both regular and firefly views to trace its course. A distinct plane is visible between the superior border of the duct and the gallbladder. The firefly view highlights the duct's separation from the gallbladder and liver, revealing its insertion into the infundibulum of the gallbladder before joining the cystic duct.

The surgeon creates a window behind the gallbladder and above the aberrant duct, continuing dissection up the gallbladder fossa to check for additional anatomical aberrancies. The anatomy is reviewed under Firefly mode, providing a clear view of the isolated aberrant duct and its relationship to the gallbladder and liver.

The surgeon performs an intraoperative cholangiogram to better understand the aberrant anatomy. A large clip is placed proximally on the apparent duct, and a 14 French angiocatheter is advanced into the abdominal cavity. A ductotomy is performed on the infundibulum of the gallbladder, proximal to the aberrant duct insertion. The first cholangiogram, with the balloon occluding the proximal system and aberrant duct, reveals the common bile duct, left hepatic duct, and at least one right hepatic duct. The second cholangiogram isolates the aberrant duct, showing significant branching before it becomes indistinct. Distal clips are placed on the infundibulum to preserve the communication of the aberrant duct with the cystic duct.

This patient has done very well post-operatively. She had no complications and is now one month from her operation. Her pathology did not reveal any malignancies. I hope this video was helpful. As always, thank you again for watching.
